title: My Spiritual Practice Is Mine; Their Judgment Isn't
subtitle: A three-minute affirmation for the person who has been told their spiritual life is too much, too little, or the wrong shape
excerpt: A three-minute affirmation for the person whose spiritual practice has been judged: by family, by old friends, by the version of themselves from ten years ago. Used in the days after a hard conversation or a sideways comment.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The mine-not-theirs affirmation shape is a staple of writing on faith and on self-trust.

practice.whenToUse: Use in the day or week after someone has judged your spiritual practice, openly or sideways. Also good before a family visit where the topic might come up.

content.text: A three-minute affirmation for the person whose spiritual life has been judged. The affirmation is short. It is said three times slow, hand on the heart.
New to affirmation as a practice? Read

Many of us have someone in the family or the friendship group who finds our spiritual practice odd. They think it is too religious, or not religious enough. Too woo, or too dry. Too public, or too quiet.
Their reading of your practice is theirs. Yours is yours. The affirmation marks the line.
My spiritual practice is mine. Their reading of it is theirs. I can let theirs be theirs and keep mine.
Sit somewhere quiet. Hand on the heart. Say the affirmation three times, slow, out loud or in your mind. The third time can be the slowest.
Use it as needed: the morning after a hard conversation, the hour before a family meal, the week of a visit. It can be said as often as it is useful.
Original to homemade.education. The mine-not-theirs shape is a staple of writing on faith and on self-trust.
